$schema_note: Security definition — authentication, authorization, QR anti-fraud, OWASP mapping, auditing, and policies

students.provider: OSAS API single sign-on — university credentials verified server-to-server; portal never stores student passwords
students.eligibility_gate: OSAS status must be active/current; alumni and inactive students rejected at login (BR-13)
students.session_issuance: On success: access JWT (15 min) + rotating refresh token (7 days)

staff.provider: Local accounts, argon2id password hashing (memory 64MB, iterations 3, parallelism 4)
staff.lockout: 5 failed attempts → 15 min lock (persisted, not in-memory); all attempts written to login logs
staff.reset: Single-use emailed token, 30 min expiry, invalidated on use or new request
staff.otp_2fa: Optional email OTP for SUPER_ADMIN / DEPT_ADMIN (settings-controlled)

tokens.access: JWT HS256 (15 min), kept in browser memory only — never localStorage
tokens.refresh: Random 256-bit value, SHA-256 hash stored, httpOnly + Secure + SameSite=Lax cookie scoped to /api/v1/auth
tokens.rotation: Every refresh issues a new token and revokes the old; reuse of a revoked token revokes the entire token family (theft detection)
tokens.device_tracking: Each refresh token stores device/user-agent + IP; users can list and revoke their device sessions

authorization.enforcement_chain: authenticate → authorize(roles) → service-level ownership guard (faculty↔assigned offering, student↔self)
principles: Backend is sole authority (frontend guards are UX only)
No self-escalation — role changes are Super Admin actions, audited

threats_and_mitigations.screenshot_sharing: Dynamic QR regenerates every 60 seconds; payload is time-limited — shared screenshots expire within the minute and fail server validation
threats_and_mitigations.replay: Single-use per student per session (DB unique constraint); token window check (±1 of 60s)
threats_and_mitigations.payload_tampering: AES-256-GCM encryption + auth tag — clients cannot read or forge payloads; keys server-side only
threats_and_mitigations.proxy_scanning_other_class: Enrollment check — only students enrolled in the offering can be recorded
threats_and_mitigations.clock_tampering: Server timestamps are authoritative; client time ignored
threats_and_mitigations.brute_force_scanning: Rate limit 10 scans/min per user; invalid payloads logged
threats_and_mitigations.session_hijack: QR issuance endpoint restricted to the owning faculty; session secret never leaves the server

qr_anti_fraud.static_qr_exception: Static mode is per-session, requires admin allowance in settings, and is flagged reduced-security in data and reports (BR-16)
qr_anti_fraud.accepted_limitations: No GPS or classroom verification by design (SystemDefinition4); physical presence enforcement relies on QR rotation speed + faculty supervision

owasp_top10_mapping.A01_broken_access_control: RBAC middleware + ownership guards + deny-by-default; tested per role in integration suite
owasp_top10_mapping.A02_cryptographic_failures: TLS 1.2+; argon2id; AES-256-GCM QR payloads; SHA-256 refresh hashes; no sensitive data in JWT claims
owasp_top10_mapping.A03_injection: Prisma parameterized queries only (no raw SQL); zod validation with unknown-key stripping; React auto-escaping for XSS
owasp_top10_mapping.A04_insecure_design: Threat-modeled QR flow (above); business rules BR-01…BR-16 enforced server-side
owasp_top10_mapping.A05_security_misconfiguration: helmet headers + strict CSP; CORS allowlist; prod configs in env vars; debug routes disabled in production
owasp_top10_mapping.A06_vulnerable_components: npm audit + Dependabot in CI; lockfile pinning; minimal dependency footprint
owasp_top10_mapping.A07_auth_failures: Lockout, rotation, reuse detection, device tracking, OSAS eligibility gate
owasp_top10_mapping.A08_integrity_failures: GCM auth tags on QR payloads; signed CI artifacts; append-only audit logs
owasp_top10_mapping.A09_logging_failures: Structured pino logs with request IDs; login/audit/attendance/sync logs; alerts on anomaries (lockout spikes, sync failures)
owasp_top10_mapping.A10_ssrf: Outbound HTTP restricted to configured OSAS/SMTP/storage hosts; no user-supplied URLs fetched

api_protection.headers: helmet defaults; HSTS (prod); CSP allowing only self + data: images
api_protection.cors: Explicit origin allowlist per environment; credentials enabled only for the SPA origin
api_protection.csrf: SameSite=Lax cookie + Bearer-header requirement on state-changing routes (cookie alone never authorizes a mutation)
api_protection.uploads: MIME + magic-byte validation; images ≤2MB (jpg/png/webp), attachments ≤5MB (pdf/jpg/png); randomized names; served from isolated path with no execute permissions
api_protection.osas_channel: Server-to-server only over TLS; API credentials in env vars; responses schema-validated (zod) before persistence; timeouts + circuit breaker with admin alerting

audit_and_logging.audit_logs: Every mutating action: actor, action (entity.verb), entity id, before/after jsonb, IP — append-only
audit_and_logging.login_logs: All attempts (success/failure/lockout) with identifier, IP, user-agent
audit_and_logging.attendance_logs: Scans (valid + rejected with rejection reason), overrides, manual entries, corrections
audit_and_logging.sync_logs: OSAS sync runs with counts and error details
audit_and_logging.review: Admin dashboard surfaces recent audit events; logs retained 5 academic years

data_protection.at_rest: Provider disk encryption; password hashes (argon2id); token hashes (SHA-256); QR secrets bytea, never serialized
data_protection.privacy: Student data limited to academic operations; profile photos from OSAS displayed only to authorized roles; no data sold or shared externally; deletion requests honored per institutional policy
data_protection.backups: Encrypted daily dumps, 30-day retention + weekly offsite; restore drills each semester (see ../maintenance)

secure_development.secrets: .env per environment, never committed; .env.example documents keys; production secrets in host secret store
secure_development.code_review: All changes via PR review; security-sensitive paths (auth, qr, attendance) require owner review
